admin.sandboxDescription: Platform-wide defaults for per-account agent-runner pods. An account inherits these unless it has a per-account override (Accounts view). Changes apply on a pod's next restart — running runners are never force-restarted.

admin.sandboxNetworkEgressHint: Coming in a later phase — runners currently have unrestricted egress.

settings.quickActionsDesc: Create shortcut buttons that appear below the chat input. Use {variable_name} in prompts to create fillable placeholders — click a quick action to populate the input, then Tab between variables to fill them in.
settings.apiKeyDesc: Use this key to authenticate API requests as your user. The key can be used in the
settings.regenerateMsg: This will invalidate your current key. Any integrations using the old key will stop working.
settings.revokeMsg: This will permanently revoke your API key. Any integrations using this key will stop working.
settings.visionModelDesc: When set, image inputs will be processed using this model instead of the default.
settings.visionModelSetupHint: Optional: select a vision model for image input support.
settings.name: Name
settings.transportNote: WebSocket is recommended for multi-worker deployments. Use SSE if WebSocket is blocked by your network.
settings.systemPromptDesc: Configure a global system prompt appended to all agent runs. Injected via the Claude Code preset mode. Applies to all users.
settings.historyRetentionDesc: Auto-delete scheduler run history older than the specified number of days. Set to 0 to disable auto-cleanup.
settings.cliPathDesc: Custom path to the Claude CLI binary. Leave empty to use the default from PATH.
settings.toolRetryDesc: Configure MCP tools that should be automatically retried by the scheduler daemon when they fail during agent runs.
settings.retryCallbackScriptDesc: Optional script invoked after each retry completes. Receives tool_name, tool_input, tool_output, session_id as arguments.
settings.retryWecomDesc: Send retry results as enterprise WeChat messages to specified users or groups.
settings.wecomDesc: Connect a WeCom AIBot to enable messaging through Enterprise WeChat. Users in the allowlist can chat with your Priva AI agent directly.
settings.wecomProxyHint: Leave empty for direct connection to WeCom. Use a WebSocket proxy when direct access to WeCom is blocked.
settings.wecomAccessAllDesc: Anyone who can DM the bot may use it.
settings.wecomAccessAllowedDesc: Only users in the list below may DM the bot.
settings.wecomAccessPrivateDesc: Only the user whose id matches the account name (the owner).
settings.wecomAccessGroupHint: Group chats ignore this setting — any member can @-trigger the bot.
settings.wecomAllowedHint: Press Enter to add. Empty list = everyone allowed.
settings.wecomModelHint: Leave empty to use your default model
settings.wecomDisconnectConfirm: This will disconnect the WeCom bot. Active conversations will be interrupted.
settings.wecomNoUsers: No allowed users configured. All enterprise users can currently chat with the bot. Add user IDs to restrict access.
settings.wecomDaemonOffline: Channel daemon is offline. Contact your administrator.
settings.feishuDesc: Feishu bot integration is under development. Stay tuned.
settings.openclawDesc: Delegate tasks to specialist agents managed by an OpenClaw gateway.
settings.openclawDisconnectConfirm: This will disconnect the OpenClaw bridge. Active delegations will be interrupted.
settings.pluginsDesc: Manage plugins that hook into the agent lifecycle. Plugins can inject information into system prompts, modify agent behavior, and more.
settings.pluginEnterpriseUserInfoDesc: Retrieves enterprise user information from MCP server and injects it into the system prompt.

webTerminal.title: Web Terminal
webTerminal.desc: Drop into a real shell on the server. The shell runs as the priva process user, has access to whatever the daemon can see, and is audited end-to-end.
webTerminal.warning: DANGER: enabling this gives every authenticated user full shell access as the priva process user. There is no privilege drop, no chroot, and no command sandbox. Only enable on systems where this risk is acceptable.
webTerminal.invalidNumber: All limits must be valid numbers

settings.fileManagerPdfNotSupported: PDF preview not supported in this browser.

hooks.selectHook: Select a hook to view details
hooks.selectHookHint: or click a node in the lifecycle graph
hooks.comingSoon: Custom handlers for this event coming soon
hooks.sensitivePatternsDesc: Regex patterns applied to mask PII in API-key client streams. Browser UI users (JWT) see unmasked data.
hooks.piiMaskBeforeModel: Mask PII before model sees output
hooks.piiMaskBeforeModelDesc: When on, sensitive data is replaced via a PostToolUse hook before tool output reaches Claude. When off, masking only applies on the way to your browser.
hooks.sensitiveTestPlaceholder: Enter text to test masking, e.g. 13912345678
hooks.deleteHandlerMessage: Remove this handler? This cannot be undone.
hooks.dryRunHint: Test command hooks or built-in hooks
hooks.passedHint: Hook allowed the operation — no block or modification
hooks.descSetup: Runs once when the agent process starts, before any session begins
hooks.descSessionStart: Fires when a session begins (startup, resume, clear, or compact)
hooks.descSessionEnd: Fires when a session ends (clear, logout, or process exit)
hooks.descUserPromptSubmit: Intercepts user input before it reaches the agent. Can modify or block the prompt
hooks.descPreToolUse: Runs before a tool is executed. Can modify parameters or block the call
hooks.descPermissionRequest: Fires when a tool needs explicit permission to run
hooks.descPostToolUse: Runs after a tool completes successfully
hooks.descPostToolUseFailure: Runs after a tool execution fails
hooks.descSubagentStart: Fires when a subagent is spawned
hooks.descSubagentStop: Fires when a subagent completes or is stopped
hooks.descStop: Intercepts the agent stop signal. Can block to keep the agent running
hooks.descNotification: Fires when the agent sends a notification
hooks.descPreCompact: Runs before conversation history is compacted
hooks.usageSetup:
Scenario: Initialize environment before any session begins.
Example: Load API keys from a vault, set up logging, or configure database connections.
Use case: Ensure all required resources are available before the agent starts processing requests.
hooks.usageSessionStart:
Scenario: React to a new or resumed session.
Example: Load user preferences, restore conversation context, or track session analytics.
Use case: Personalize behavior based on how the session was started (startup vs resume vs compact).
hooks.usageSessionEnd:
Scenario: Clean up when a session ends.
Example: Flush logs, save session state, release temp files, or notify monitoring services.
Use case: Ensure graceful shutdown and proper resource cleanup regardless of exit reason.
hooks.usageUserPromptSubmit:
Scenario: Validate or transform user input before the agent processes it. Can block.
Example: Strip PII from prompts, enforce content policies, or inject additional context.
Use case: Add a compliance layer that sanitizes every user message before it reaches the model.
hooks.usagePreToolUse:
Scenario: Intercept tool calls before execution. Can modify params or block. Matches tool name.
Example: Prevent file writes to protected dirs, add audit logging, or redirect paths per environment.
Use case: Enforce security policies — e.g., block Bash commands containing 'rm -rf' or 'sudo'.
hooks.usagePermissionRequest:
Scenario: Auto-approve or deny tool permissions based on custom rules. Can block.
Example: Auto-allow read-only file operations, require approval for network calls.
Use case: Build a fine-grained permission system that replaces manual approval with policy-based rules.
hooks.usagePostToolUse:
Scenario: Process tool results after successful execution. Matches tool name.
Example: Parse output for metrics, cache results, or trigger follow-up actions.
Use case: Collect usage statistics — log every tool call's duration, input size, and output for analytics.
hooks.usagePostToolUseFailure:
Scenario: Handle tool failures with custom recovery logic. Matches tool name.
Example: Retry with different parameters, fall back to alternative tools, or alert on critical failures.
Use case: Auto-retry failed API calls with exponential backoff before surfacing the error.
hooks.usageSubagentStart:
Scenario: Configure or track subagent creation. Matches agent type.
Example: Assign resource limits, inject specialized system prompts, or log spawning for cost tracking.
Use case: Apply per-agent-type budgets or add tracing headers for distributed observability.
hooks.usageSubagentStop:
Scenario: Process subagent results or clean up. Matches agent type.
Example: Aggregate outputs from multiple subagents, release allocated resources, or update progress.
Use case: Merge results from parallel research subagents into a single coherent response.
hooks.usageStop:
Scenario: Intercept agent shutdown. Can block to keep the agent running.
Example: Ensure pending operations complete, prompt for confirmation, or auto-save work in progress.
Use case: Prevent premature shutdown — keep the agent alive until all queued tasks finish.
hooks.usageNotification:
Scenario: Customize notification delivery. Matches notification type.
Example: Route to Slack, email, or webhooks based on severity or content.
Use case: Send high-priority notifications to PagerDuty while routing info-level ones to a Slack channel.
hooks.usagePreCompact:
Scenario: Preserve or modify data before context window compaction.
Example: Extract important facts to external memory, prioritize which context to retain.
Use case: Save key decisions and code references to a knowledge base before they're compacted away.

quote.template:
<quote-content>
The user have quote a part of the content in your response:`{{content}}`
and give the following feedback, cross check with your response, and provide targeted action/suggestions if possible
</quote-content>
{{feedback}}

welcome.title: Welcome to Priva
welcome.description: Priva is an autonomous coding and task agent built on the Claude Agent SDK. Drive it from chat, extend it with skills, and schedule it to run on its own.

chat.title: Start a conversation
chat.description: Open the Priva tab, pick a model in the header, and send your first prompt. Long-running tasks stream progress into the canvas panel on the right.

quickActions.title: Use Quick Actions
quickActions.description: Set up Quick Actions in Settings, then use the shortcut buttons below the composer to insert reusable prompt templates and jump through the filled {variables} before sending.

power.title: Enhance your input
power.description: Bring richer context into every prompt: drag and drop images into the composer, tap + to attach files, type / to trigger a skill, and switch models inline without leaving your input.

permissionMode.title: Choose a permission mode
permissionMode.description: Use the shield menu under the composer to control how much autonomy Priva has. Switch to Plan when you want it to draft the approach and wait for review before making edits.

feedback.title: Answer Priva's questions
feedback.description: When Priva needs clarification, it can raise an interactive question in chat. Choose answers or type a note, then Priva sends that structured reply back into the conversation and continues from it.

structuredExecution.title: Structured Execution Messages
structuredExecution.description: Priva turns an agent run into a traceable message flow. Thoughts, tool calls, subagents, and outcomes appear in order so you can inspect the full execution path.

interactiveRunning.title: Interactive While Running
interactiveRunning.description: When the agent starts drifting, Priva can ask for a quick correction mid-run and continue on the right path without restarting the whole execution.

rewindBranch.title: Message-Level Rewind / Branch
rewindBranch.description: With file checkpoints enabled, rewind changes from a specific message or branch from that point into a new session to try a safer path.

canvasLinkage.title: Chat + Canvas Linkage
canvasLinkage.description: Todos, file edits, and plans from the conversation sync into the Canvas on the right, so you can follow execution state without leaving chat.

pluginSetup.title: Set up plugins
pluginSetup.description: Open Settings, head to Plugins, and wire an enabled plugin to an MCP provider by filling its URL, tool name, timeout, and headers.

skillSetup.title: Install and refine skills
skillSetup.description: Use Skill Hub or uploads to add skills, browse their files, switch between Preview and Source, then ask Priva to optimize selected sections in plan mode.

mcpSetup.title: Connect MCP tools
mcpSetup.description: Add a project or global MCP server, test it before saving, inspect and debug its tools, review prompts and resources, then choose which servers are available in chat.

quickActions.createShortcutDesc: Create shortcut buttons that appear below the chat input.
quickActions.bugReportPrompt: Summarize issue, impact, and next steps

pluginSetup.pluginDescription: Hook MCP results into the agent lifecycle before each run.

skillSetup.hubSkillDesc: Draft polished release notes with placeholders for feature and audience.
skillSetup.sourceLine3: Start with the user impact, then summarize shipped changes.
skillSetup.sourceLine4: Call out risks, migrations, and any required follow-up steps.
skillSetup.commentText: Clarify the placeholders, tighten the output format, and keep it reviewable.
skillSetup.handoffDetail: Priva will read the current skill, compare your notes, and ask for review before editing.

mcpSetup.resourceDesc: Resolved design-system docs for the selected component.
mcpSetup.toolDesc: Search internal documentation and return matching references.

permission.tipPre: Pick a permission mode before you start. Use
permission.tipPost: when you want Priva to draft the approach and wait for review first.
permission.planReviewDesc: Priva will stop and wait for your confirmation before editing files.
permission.userPrompt: Refactor the auth flow but keep the existing API stable.

feedback.clarificationMsg: I need one quick clarification before I start setting up the environment.
feedback.goalsQuestion: What should Priva optimize for while setting up the environment?
feedback.option1Desc: Use a clean worktree or contained setup changes.
feedback.option2Desc: Stay in the current workspace and move quickly.
feedback.option3Desc: Keep me updated while Priva runs the setup steps.
feedback.skip: Skip — I have something to say
feedback.answerText: - Goals -> Keep edits isolated - Use a clean worktree or contained setup changes.; Share progress as it works - Keep me updated while Priva runs the setup steps.; Custom: Preserve existing API keys

structuredExecution.userPrompt: Audit the auth flow and outline the safest fix.
structuredExecution.assistantLead: Tracing the run in structured steps so you can inspect every decision before I finish the patch.
structuredExecution.thinkingLine: Comparing entrypoints, recent edits, and likely risk areas…
structuredExecution.readDesc: Open the current routes, guards, and session helpers first.
structuredExecution.planDesc: Break the fix into reviewable steps before editing files.
structuredExecution.subagentDesc: Delegate session edge-case review without losing the parent trace.
structuredExecution.subagentToolDesc: Confirm the expiry branch still matches the current API contract.
structuredExecution.todoDesc: Mirror progress so the next file edit is easy to follow.
structuredExecution.finalNote: Every meaningful step stays in chat as a structured execution trail, not a single opaque reply.

interactiveRunning.userPrompt: Refactor the auth middleware, but avoid touching billing routes.
interactiveRunning.runningStepDesc: Priva is still running and keeps the existing context alive.
interactiveRunning.wrongLabel: Path drifting toward shared route config
interactiveRunning.wrongDesc: A quick correction is better than stopping the whole run and starting over.
interactiveRunning.questionText: Should I stay in the shared router, or limit changes to auth-only files and continue from there?
interactiveRunning.option1Desc: Continue through the broader route config.
interactiveRunning.option2Label: Constrain the fix to auth-only files
interactiveRunning.option2Desc: Correct the direction now and keep the run going.
interactiveRunning.answerSummary: Auth-only files. Keep the run alive and continue from there.
interactiveRunning.correctedDesc: Pivot the current execution path without resetting the conversation.
interactiveRunning.continueDesc: Priva resumes the same run and carries the correction forward.

rewindBranch.userPrompt: Apply the auth patch and keep the existing response shape stable.
rewindBranch.fileDesc: The change landed from this assistant turn and can be targeted directly.
rewindBranch.confirmTitle: Rewind files to before this turn?
rewindBranch.confirmBody: Restore files from the saved checkpoint for this message while keeping the conversation history intact.
rewindBranch.bannerText: Files restored from this message checkpoint.
rewindBranch.branchHint: Fork from the same message if you want to try another path.

canvasLinkage.userPrompt: Plan the auth fix, patch it, and show me the risky files.
canvasLinkage.assistantLead: As the run progresses, Priva syncs the plan, file edits, and status into Canvas beside the chat.
canvasLinkage.todoDesc: The task list appears in chat and mirrors into Inspector.
canvasLinkage.fileDesc: Changed files appear in the Files tab as the run writes them.
canvasLinkage.planDesc: When a plan is produced, the Plan tab becomes the review surface.
canvasLinkage.filePanelDesc: Edit recorded from the current conversation turn.
canvasLinkage.planPanelBody:
1. Confirm API contract
2. Narrow auth changes
3. Patch session path
4. Review risky files
